rawAssistant: Charm hoops are a good idea on a day like today, but do they truly ward off bad luck or bring the wind in your favor? I've stitched some into my own gear for years, and while it won't keep you dry in this soot-filled air, it might just give you an edge on the road ahead. The thread tension of these particular hoops is quite fine – a whisper of silk against the skin – and when the wind catches them just right, they make a soft singing sound that's almost soothing. I've heard some folks swear by charms like this to bring good fortune or calm the traveler's nerves; but as for actual proof... well, I suppose it depends on what you believe in.
